2606.32023.title: FLORA: A deep learning approach to predict forest attributes from heterogeneous LiDAR data
2606.32023.paper_summary: Forest attributes are essential for national-scale resource monitoring. Airborne LiDAR metrics are among the auxiliary variables most strongly correlated with forest attributes used in National Forest Inventory (NFI) estimates. However, producing wall-to-wall predictions remains challenging when LiDAR data are acquired under heterogeneous conditions. As national LiDAR programs expand across Europe, variability in sensors, flight parameters, seasons, and scan angles limits the robustness of existing models, which are often calibrated for local conditions. We present FLORA (Forest LiDAR Octree Regression with Auxiliary Data), a deep learning framework that predicts six forest attributes: dominant height, total volume, deciduous volume, coniferous volume, basal area, and stem density from heterogeneous LiDAR point clouds. FLORA combines an octree-based backbone with ecological and spatiotemporal auxiliary variables through a late-fusion gating mechanism. Models are trained and evaluated on 32,052 National Forest Inventory plots across mainland France using data from the French LiDAR HD program. A single model trained on both leaf-on and leaf-off acquisitions outperforms season-specific models and improves cross-season robustness. Auxiliary variables provide modest overall gains but contribute more strongly to species-specific volume prediction. FLORA achieves an rRMSE of about 12.3% (R2 = 0.88) for dominant height and 39% (R2 = 0.74) for total volume, providing a robust baseline for large-scale forest attribute estimation from heterogeneous national LiDAR programs.
2606.32023.paper_summary_zh: 森林特徵對於國家級資源監測至關重要。空中LiDAR指標是與國家森林清查（NFI）估算中使用的森林特徵最強相關的輔助變量之一。 然而，當LiDAR數據在異質條件下獲取時，產生全面的預測仍然具有挑戰性。 隨著國家LiDAR計劃在歐洲的擴展，傳感器、飛行參數、季節和掃描角度的變異性限制了現有模型的穩健性，這些模型通常是針對當地條件進行校準的。 我們提出了FLORA（森林LiDAR八叉樹回歸與輔助數據），這是一個深度學習框架，可以從異質的LiDAR點雲中預測六個森林特徵：主導高度、總體積、落葉體積、針葉體積、基面積和幹密度。 FLORA通過晚融合閘機制將基於八叉樹的骨幹與生態和時空輔助變量相結合。 模型在法國本土的32,052個國家森林清查樣地上進行訓練和評估，使用來自法國LiDAR HD計劃的數據。 在葉子生長和落葉的數據上訓練的單一模型表現優於特定季節的模型，並提高了跨季節的穩健性。 輔助變量提供了適度的整體增益，但對於特定物種的體積預測貢獻更大。 FLORA對主導高度的相對均方根誤差（rRMSE）約為12.3%（R2 = 0.88），對總體積為39%（R2 = 0.74），為從異質國家LiDAR計劃中進行大規模森林特徵估算提供了穩健的基準。
2606.32023.author: Emilie Vautier et.al.
2606.32023.authors: Emilie Vautier, Clément Mallet, Cédric Vega
